message:
test(allowlist): add ISO 8601 no-timezone expiration tests

Add tests for is_expired() handling of ISO 8601 timestamps without timezone
(e.g., "2020-01-01T00:00:00"). These are treated as UTC per the existing code.

- entry_with_future_iso8601_no_tz_is_not_expired
- entry_with_past_iso8601_no_tz_is_expired

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -11,7 +11,7 @@
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.3","title":"Allowlist CLI: add/list/remove/validate (project + user)","description":"## Objective

Implement `dcg allowlist` CLI commands so users can fix false positives without editing TOML by hand.

## Commands (v1)

Primary namespace:

- `dcg allowlist add <rule_id> --reason "..." [--project|--user] [--expires ...] [--condition KEY=VAL]`
- `dcg allowlist add-command "<cmd>" --reason "..." [--project|--user] [--expires ...]`
- `dcg allowlist list [--project|--user]`
- `dcg allowlist remove <rule_id|entry_id> [--project|--user]`
- `dcg allowlist validate [--project|--user]`

Ergonomic aliases (high-value):

- `dcg allow <rule_id> --reason "..." [--project|--user]` → alias of `allowlist add`
- `dcg unallow <rule_id|entry_id> [--project|--user]` → alias of `allowlist remove`

Rationale: deny messages can print shorter copy/paste commands.

## Behavioral requirements

- Default scope should be explicit:
  - either require `--project|--user`, or default to project if in a git repo and file exists
- All mutations must preserve TOML readability (stable ordering; keep comments where feasible).
- CLI should set audit metadata automatically:
  - `added_by` (from env/user)
  - `added_at` (RFC3339)

## Acceptance criteria

- CLI can create `.dcg/allowlist.toml` if missing.
- CLI mutations are idempotent and stable (no reordering chaos).
- Validate prints actionable errors/warnings.

## Tests

- CLI integration tests for add/list/remove (including alias commands).
- Validation tests for:
  - expired entries
  - missing reason
  - unknown rule id

All quality gates pass (463 tests, clippy, fmt).","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:04:59.51609388-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:21:56.560137459-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.4","title":"Allowlist: conditions, expiration, and wildcard safety rules","description":"## Objective

Implement the more advanced allowlist semantics in a way that is safe and predictable.

## Semantics to support

### 1) Expiration

- `expires_at` field on entries.
- Expired entries:
  - evaluator: ignored
  - validate: warning (or error if `--strict`)

### 2) Conditions (environment gating)

- `conditions = { KEY = "VAL" }` maps to environment variables.
- Conditions are ANDed.
- Missing env var means condition not met.

### 3) Wildcards

- Allow `pack_id:*` (scoped to one pack).
- Disallow `*:*`.
- Allow `pack_id:prefix-*` only if we commit to stable naming conventions.

### 4) Risk acknowledgement

- Regex allowlist entries require `risk_acknowledged=true`.
- Validation should warn on overly broad regex (heuristics).

## Acceptance criteria

- Expiration works and is test-covered.
- Conditions work (CI-only allowlists etc).
- Wildcard constraints prevent unsafe global bypass.

## Tests

- Expiration tests using fixed timestamps.
- Condition tests with env var manipulation.
- Wildcard constraint tests.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.5","title":"Allowlist: comprehensive tests + documentation","description":"## Objective

Make allowlisting safe in practice by shipping:

- comprehensive automated tests
- clear user documentation

## Tests

### Parser/validation

- invalid TOML does not crash hook
- missing required fields surfaced in validate
- risky entries require acknowledgement

### Evaluator integration

- allowlist overrides only the intended rule
- allowlisting does not disable other packs
- allowlist layer precedence works

### CLI

- add/list/remove idempotence
- validate output contains actionable messages

## Docs

- README section: "Resolving false positives" describing:
  - `dcg explain`
  - `dcg allowlist add ...`
  - recommended allowlist shapes
  - security caveats

## Acceptance criteria

- Tests cover the core allowlist behaviors and guard against regressions.
- Docs are sufficient that a user can resolve a false positive without reading source.

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.6","title":"Allowlist: E2E coverage in scripts/e2e_test.sh (hook path)","description":"## Objective

Add end-to-end coverage proving allowlists affect the **real hook path** (stdin JSON → decision) and do not create accidental broad bypasses.

## Why E2E is required

Allowlisting is a high-risk feature:

- It must override only the intended rule.
- It must work in hook mode (not just `dcg test`).
- It must not silently disable packs or normalization.

## Test cases (must include)

1) Baseline: a known catastrophic command is blocked
- e.g., `git reset --hard` (record the rule_id used)

2) Project allowlist overrides that exact rule
- Create `.dcg/allowlist.toml` in an isolated fixture repo/cwd.
- Re-run the same hook JSON input.
- Expected: ALLOW (or WARN if configured) with **no JSON deny**.

3) Non-target rule remains enforced
- Ensure another blocked command is still blocked (e.g., `git clean -fd`).

4) Expired allowlist entry does not apply

5) Unsafe wildcard entry is rejected (or requires risk_ack)

## Logging requirements

E2E output should print:

- the rule_id being allowlisted
- which allowlist layer was used (project vs user)
- expected vs actual decision

## Acceptance criteria

- E2E fails if allowlist does not apply in hook mode.
- E2E fails if allowlist is broader than intended.

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.2.2` evaluator integration
- `git_safety_guard-1gt.2.3` allowlist CLI (optional; direct file edit acceptable in test)

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3","title":"Add decision modes: block vs warn vs log-only","description":"## Objective

Reduce disruption by allowing less-confident detections to warn or log instead of hard-blocking, while keeping high-confidence matches as hard blocks.

## Background

Some detections are extremely high confidence (e.g., `rm -rf /`, `git reset --hard`). Others are context-dependent or susceptible to false positives.

A single binary mode (always deny) can be overly disruptive, especially when expanding into heredoc scanning and AST-based heuristics.

## Proposed behavior

Add a policy layer that can choose between:

- **deny** (current behavior): block command
- **warn**: print warning to stderr and/or log, but allow command (no JSON deny)
- **log-only**: silent allow, but log event for later review

Policy should be configurable:

- global default (e.g., deny)
- per-pack override
- per-pattern override

## Safety constraints

- Default should remain deny for known-catastrophic patterns.
- Warn/log-only must never apply to the most dangerous patterns unless the user explicitly configures it.

## Acceptance criteria

- Config supports selecting mode per pack/pattern.
- Output is correct per mode:
  - deny: stderr warning + stdout JSON deny
  - warn: stderr warning, no stdout
  - log-only: optional log entry, no stderr/stdout
- Tests cover all three modes.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:05:08.791980925-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T14:19:44.235316204-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T14:19:44.235316204-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented policy config (default/pack/rule) and hook behavior for deny/warn/log; added E2E policy coverage; Critical rules cannot be globally downgraded.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:05:08.793291122-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:06:02.265838837-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.1","title":"Decision policy: define rule severity taxonomy + default modes","description":"## Objective

Define an explicit, documented severity taxonomy for rules, and map severity → default decision mode.

This prevents ad-hoc "some things warn, some deny" drift and supports:

- predictable rollout (warn-first where appropriate)
- stable CI policy (`--fail-on error|warning` for scan)
- consistent UX between hook, explain, and scan

## Proposed taxonomy (v1)

- `error` (catastrophic / irreversible): default **DENY**
  - examples: `rm -rf /`, `git reset --hard`, `git clean -fd`, `DROP DATABASE prod`

- `warning` (dangerous but sometimes intentional): default **WARN** (or DENY in strict packs)
  - examples: `git push --force` (could be warn if confidence low)

- `info` (advisory): default **LOG-ONLY**

## Rules of the road

- Severity is a property of a rule (pack_id + pattern_name), not of user configuration.
- User configuration can *tighten* (warn→deny) freely.
- User configuration can *loosen* (deny→warn/log) only with explicit acknowledgement (see allowlist safety rules).

## Acceptance criteria

- `Severity` enum exists and is used consistently across:
  - hook deny/warn/log output
  - `dcg explain`
  - `dcg scan` JSON output
- Default mapping is documented and test-covered.

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.3` (decision modes implementation)
- `git_safety_guard-99e.2` (stable rule IDs)
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:01:58.171989907-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T01:50:42.026890924-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T01:50:42.026890924-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented Severity and DecisionMode enums with Critical/High/Medium/Low levels. Updated DestructivePattern, DestructiveMatch, CheckResult structs. Added 10 comprehensive tests. All 92 tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:02:07.49512402-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:02:19.24160358-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.2","title":"Decision policy: tag existing rules with severity + regression tests","description":"## Objective

Apply the severity taxonomy from `git_safety_guard-1gt.3.1` to existing packs and ensure outputs stay stable.

## Scope

- Core packs (`core.git`, `core.filesystem`) first.
- High-signal packs next (docker/k8s/db) as they become reachable in hook mode.

## Requirements

- Each destructive rule has an explicit severity.
- Scan output includes severity for each finding.
- Deny/warn/log mode defaults respect severity mapping.

## Tests

- Snapshot tests for:
  - deny output includes severity
  - explain JSON includes severity
  - scan JSON includes severity
- Regression tests for key rules:
  - `core.git:reset-hard` remains `error`
  - `core.filesystem:rm-rf-non-temp` remains `error`

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.3.1` (taxonomy)
- `git_safety_guard-99e.2.2` (pattern metadata propagation)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Every rule in core packs has an explicit severity tag.
- Hook output includes severity (or decision mode derived from severity) in a stable, test-covered way.
- `dcg explain` shows severity per match and per final decision.
- `dcg scan --json` emits severity per finding and honors `--fail-on error|warning`.
- Regression tests prevent accidental severity drift for a curated set of high-impact rules.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:02:32.98122712-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T13:48:33.789940747-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T13:48:33.789940747-05:00","close_reason":"Completed severity tagging for core packs and explain output","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:02:42.227156139-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:02:54.842192671-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:02:59.882469179-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
@@ -114,7 +114,7 @@
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.1","title":"scan: CLI args and command surface","description":"## Objective

Implement the `dcg scan` CLI argument parsing and subcommand UX.

This task is about **CLI surface area and flag semantics**, not the extractor implementations.

## Command selection modes (mutually exclusive)

- `dcg scan --staged`
- `dcg scan --paths <paths...>`
- `dcg scan --git-diff <rev-range>`

## Output / policy flags

- `--format pretty|json`
- `--fail-on error|warning|none`
  - `error`: non-zero exit if any *error/deny* finding exists
  - `warning`: non-zero exit if any warn/deny exists
  - `none`: always exit 0

## Safety / performance knobs

- `--max-file-size <bytes>` (default conservative, e.g. 1MB)
- `--max-findings <n>` (cap total findings)
- `--exclude <glob>` (repeatable)
- `--include <glob>` (repeatable; optional)
- `--redact <mode>` (e.g., `none|quoted|aggressive`) and/or `--truncate <n>`

## UX flags

- `--verbose` (include skipped-file reasons, extractor stats)
- `--top <n>` (limit printed exemplars in pretty output)

## Error handling

- Invalid flag combos produce actionable errors:
  - cannot combine `--staged` and `--paths`
  - cannot combine `--staged` and `--git-diff`
  - missing `rev-range` for `--git-diff`

## Acceptance criteria

- `dcg scan --help` clearly explains modes and exit codes.
- Invalid flag combos produce helpful errors.
- Defaults are conservative (avoid scanning the world unintentionally).
","notes":"Implementation landed in commit 1d915d5: adds dcg scan CLI surface + handler skeleton (staged/paths/git-diff, format/fail-on/max limits/include/exclude/redaction/truncation placeholders) and unit tests for arg parsing + glob matching.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:16:03.443226256-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T13:20:23.839374506-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T13:20:23.839374506-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented scan CLI args and explain command","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:16:28.383412499-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.2","title":"scan: git integration (staged + diff file lists)","description":"## Objective

Implement file list discovery for:

- staged files (`git diff --cached --name-only`)
- diff ranges (`git diff --name-only <rev-range>`)

## Requirements

- Deterministic ordering.
- Clear errors when not in a git repo.
- Path filtering and excludes are applied consistently.

## Design notes

### Prefer NUL-separated output

To correctly handle filenames with spaces/newlines, prefer `-z` forms:

- staged: `git diff --cached --name-only -z`
- diff range: `git diff --name-only -z <rev-range>`

### Deleted / renamed files

Scanning should only attempt to read files that exist in the selected content source:

- For `--staged` scans, the *index* is the source of truth.
- For `--git-diff` scans, the working tree is usually sufficient (CI), but we must not crash on deleted paths.

Implementation should explicitly decide what to do with:

- deleted files (skip)
- renames (scan the new path)
- submodules (skip)

### Consistent filtering

All file lists must flow through one shared filter pipeline:

- include globs
- exclude globs
- max file size
- max files (optional)

## Test plan

Create a small fixture repo in unit tests and cover:

- staged add/modify
- staged rename
- staged delete (should not return deleted path)
- filenames containing spaces
- “not a git repo” (clear error)

## Acceptance Criteria

- `dcg scan --staged` returns a deterministic, de-duplicated list of staged file paths.
- `dcg scan --git-diff <rev-range>` returns a deterministic, de-duplicated list of changed file paths.
- Deleted paths do not cause crashes or noisy errors; they are skipped with an optional debug log.
- Filenames with spaces/newlines are handled correctly (use `-z` plumbing).
- Filters (include/exclude/size) apply identically regardless of file list source.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive / noise controls
- Correct file selection reduces false positives by ensuring we only scan the intended diff/staged surface.
- Deleted paths and non-text files should be skipped deterministically (with optional debug log), not treated as scan failures.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:16:08.538495954-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T13:31:02.109015085-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T13:31:02.109015085-05:00","close_reason":"Git integration complete: staged files, git-diff, NUL-separated parsing, space/newline handling, rename/delete handling, tests in place","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:16:33.475014249-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","title":"scan: output formatting + fail-on policy","description":"## Objective

Implement output formatting for scan results and the policy for failing (blocking) in CI/pre-commit.

This task defines the **stable JSON schema** and the **exit-code policy**.

## Output formats

### Pretty (human)

- Group findings by file.
- For each finding show:
  - location (file:line[:col])
  - extractor id
  - decision (ALLOW/WARN/DENY)
  - rule id (when matched)
  - short reason
  - truncated/redacted command preview
- Include a summary footer:
  - totals by decision/severity
  - total files scanned / skipped
  - elapsed time

### JSON (machine)

Schema requirements (v1):

- `schema_version: 1`
- `summary`:
  - `files_scanned`, `files_skipped`
  - `findings_total`
  - counts by `decision`
- `findings[]`:
  - `file`, `line`, optional `col`
  - `extractor_id`
  - `extracted_command` (truncated/redacted)
  - `decision`: allow|warn|deny
  - `severity`: info|warning|error
  - `rule_id` (when matched)
  - `reason` (when matched)
  - `suggestion?`

Determinism:

- Findings are sorted deterministically:
  1) `file` asc
  2) `line` asc
  3) `col` asc
  4) `rule_id` asc (or extractor_id if no rule)

## `--fail-on` policy

- `none`: always exit 0
- `warning`: exit non-zero if any `severity in {warning,error}` exists
- `error`: exit non-zero only if any `severity == error` exists

## Redaction / truncation

- Default: truncate long commands.
- `--redact` controls whether to redact quoted strings and other likely-secret spans.
- Redaction must not break JSON schema.

## Acceptance criteria

- Exit codes match documented policy.
- Outputs include rule id, severity, and reason when a match exists.
- Output ordering is stable for snapshot/golden tests.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive controls
- Output should make “executed vs data” obvious to reviewers (include extractor kind + extracted command snippet).
- Redaction/truncation should avoid leaking secrets while still being debuggable.
- `--fail-on` should default to high-confidence catastrophic rules only; warn-first for the rest.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:16:13.636737749-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T14:36:32.920265066-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T14:36:32.920265066-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented scan v1 JSON schema (schema_version + summary counts), deterministic finding ordering, pretty output grouped-by-file with extractor_id/decision/severity, and wired redact/truncate plumbing.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:16:38.567223462-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:02.730946194-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:07.828229935-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:28.214491827-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:33.308987176-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:22:33.951854137-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:22:38.998889654-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.4","title":"scan: unit tests for CLI/path selection/output","description":"## Objective

Add unit/integration tests for `dcg scan` CLI parsing, file selection boundaries, and output stability.

## Coverage goals

### CLI parsing

- mutual exclusion of `--staged`/`--paths`/`--git-diff`
- defaults are conservative
- help text includes exit code semantics

### File selection

- respects `--exclude` / `--include`
- respects `--max-file-size`
- deterministic ordering

### Output correctness

- JSON schema v1 fields exist (schema_version, summary, findings[])
- deterministic ordering of findings
- `--fail-on` exit code mapping is correct
- redaction/truncation behavior is deterministic

## Logging requirements

On failure, tests print:

- the CLI invocation used
- captured stdout/stderr (truncated)
- parsed JSON diff vs expected (for json mode)

## Acceptance criteria

- Tests fail if we regress into extracting documentation strings.
- Snapshot/golden tests remain stable across runs.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive coverage
Ensure scan unit tests include explicit “must-not-find” cases:

- dangerous substrings in comments
- dangerous substrings in string literals / docs
- safe commands in executed contexts","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:16:18.732314405-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T23:27:37.377896153-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:16:43.658703242-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:12.924866928-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:18.01864202-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:23.11472507-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3","title":"Implement MVP extractors (shell, Dockerfile, GitHub Actions, Makefile)","description":"## Objective

Implement the first set of conservative, high-signal extractors for `dcg scan`.

## Guiding principle

Only extract **executable contexts**. Avoid extracting from fields that are obviously documentation.

This task should explicitly encode these boundaries so false positives don't explode.

## MVP extractors

### 1) Shell scripts (`*.sh`)

- Extract candidate command lines from scripts.
- Keep it conservative:
  - ignore pure comments
  - ignore blank lines
  - treat each non-comment line as a candidate command line (we're not interpreting variables)
- Long term improvement (not required for v1): handle multiline `\` continuations.

### 2) Dockerfile

- Extract `RUN <shell form>` lines.
- Ignore JSON-array exec-form `RUN ["cmd", ...]` for v1 unless we add a tiny parser.
- Do NOT scan comments or `LABEL`/`ENV`/`ARG` values.

### 3) GitHub Actions workflow YAML

- Extract only `steps[].run` (string or block scalar).
- Explicitly ignore:
  - `name:` fields
  - `env:` values
  - `with:` values
  - docs/comments

### 4) Makefile

- Extract recipe lines (tab-indented).
- Handle `\` continuation for adjacent recipe lines.
- Ignore variable definitions and comments.

## Output requirements

Each extracted command must include:

- `file`, `line`, optional `col`
- `extractor_id`
- the extracted command string

## Tests

- Unit tests per extractor with minimal fixtures:
  - confirm *only* execution contexts are extracted
  - confirm correct line numbers
  - regression tests for common tricky cases (YAML block scalars, Makefile continuations)

## Acceptance criteria

- The extractors can be run on representative sample files without panicking.
- Unit tests demonstrate the extractor boundaries (no docs strings).
- Extracted commands are deterministic and stable for golden tests.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:49:26.918414721-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T20:49:26.918414721-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:56:04.621135514-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:23:31.911198902-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.1","title":"scan extractor: shell scripts (.sh)","description":"## Objective

Extract executable command contexts from shell scripts **conservatively**.

Shell scripts are noisy: many lines are assignments, control structures, or function definitions. A naive "every non-comment line" approach will create false positives.

## Rules (v1)

### 1) Ignore non-executable lines

Skip lines that are clearly not command invocations, e.g.:

- blank lines and pure comments
- variable assignments at start of line:
  - `FOO=bar`
  - `export FOO=bar`
- shell control keywords / structure:
  - `if`, `then`, `elif`, `fi`, `for`, `while`, `until`, `do`, `done`, `case`, `esac`, `{`, `}`
- function declarations:
  - `foo() {`
  - `function foo {`

### 2) Extract only likely command lines

For remaining lines:

- strip leading whitespace
- treat the line as a candidate command line
- do not attempt to evaluate variables

### 3) Line continuations (minimal support)

- If a line ends with `\`, join with the next line for extraction purposes (bounded join length).

### 4) Keyword prefilter (optional but recommended)

- If the line does not contain any enabled-pack keyword, skip extraction early.
  - This keeps extraction cheap and avoids scanning lots of irrelevant lines.

## Acceptance criteria

- Extracted commands include accurate line numbers (start line of a continued command).
- No panics on unusual Unicode or long lines.
- Does not extract obvious data-only assignments like `DOC="rm -rf"`.

## Tests

- Fixtures must include negative cases:
  - variable assignment containing dangerous substrings (must NOT extract)
  - comments mentioning dangerous substrings (must NOT extract)
  - control-structure lines (must NOT extract)
- Positive cases:
  - typical command lines (must extract)
  - continued command lines (must extract and join)
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:16:54.946988626-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T22:26:43.653987975-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:17:23.667222661-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.2","title":"scan extractor: Dockerfile RUN (shell form)","description":"## Objective

Extract executable shell commands from Dockerfile `RUN` instructions.

## Rules (v1)

- Extract `RUN <shell form>`.
- Ignore JSON exec-form `RUN ["cmd", ...]` in v1 unless we add a tiny parser.
- Ignore comments, labels, env, args.

## Acceptance criteria

- Correctly handles `RUN` with line continuations (`\\`).
- Provides correct line number for the start of the RUN command.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive controls
- Extract only Dockerfile `RUN` commands (shell form) and do not scan comments.
- Treat here-doc style `RUN <<EOF` carefully (avoid double-scanning; use a single extractor path).

### Tests
- Positive fixture: `RUN rm -rf /` (deny)
- Negative fixtures:
  - `# RUN rm -rf /` (comment)
  - `RUN echo "rm -rf"` (data-only mention)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:17:00.042137556-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T23:27:51.972817742-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:17:28.75720403-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
